task_completion.description: Partition the space with at least 4 interior walls to separate public and secure zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D floor plan view of Sweet Home 3D, look for at least 4 distinct interior wall segments drawn to divide the open building shell into separate areas.

task_completion.description: Add at least 3 doors for access between the partitioned areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 3 door swing symbols in the 2D plan or 3D door models placed within the interior or exterior walls.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Public Zone with at least 15 seats and a security screening desk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count at least 15 chairs/seats grouped in the designated public waiting area and identify one desk positioned near the entrance or partition for security screening.

task_completion.description: Construct at least 4 interview stations along the partition using 4 desks/counters and 8 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 4 desks or counters placed against or integrated into the dividing wall, accompanied by a total of 8 chairs (typically arranged with one on each side of the 4 desks).

task_completion.description: Furnish the Secure Back-Office with at least 4 desks, 4 office chairs, 6 storage units, and a break corner with 1 appliance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the secure staff area, count at least 4 desks, 4 office chairs, 6 storage units (e.g., cabinets, shelves, filing cabinets), and a break area containing at least 1 appliance (e.g., microwave, fridge).

task_completion.description: Define and label at least 4 distinct zones and apply distinct floor colors or textures to at least 2 rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 4 distinct text labels in the 2D view (e.g., 'Security', 'Public Waiting', 'Interview Booths', 'Secure Back-Office') and observe at least two distinct floor colors or textures applied to the rooms in the 3D or 2D view.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to design the floor plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window open with the agent actively dragging/dropping furniture, drawing walls, or editing room properties, rather than just editing XML files in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent placed individual furniture items rather than importing a single pre-built 3D model of a completed office.
integrity.visual_evidence: The furniture list panel on the left side of the Sweet Home 3D interface should show multiple individual items (desks, chairs, doors) rather than a single imported object.

integrity.description: Verify the agent saved the completed floor plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Save' dialog or the absence of the unsaved asterisk (*) next to the filename 'consular_annex_starter.sh3d' in the window title bar at the end of the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses 'consular_annex_starter.sh3d'. Note that the starter file natively retains 10 named rooms and 8 rooms with floor colors from a residential sample due to a setup flaw. The VLM should verify if the agent explicitly created the required consular zones (e.g., 'Security', 'Public Waiting') and applied colors, rather than just relying on the pre-existing residential rooms. Additionally, while the programmatic verifier fails to detect doors due to an XML serialization mismatch, the VLM should visually confirm the presence of at least 3 doors.
